description.value: Multi-model enterprise relational database management system, primarily SQL-based, used for mission-critical OLTP and data-warehousing workloads
description.comment: Primarily a relational DBMS with secondary document, graph, spatial and vector models.

website.comment: Official product page; derived from the vendor's primary domain.

license.comment: Proprietary/commercial license. A restricted free edition (Oracle Database Free / XE) exists, but the product itself is closed-source proprietary.

first-release.comment: Oracle V2 (1979) was the first commercially available SQL-based RDBMS.

latest-version.comment: Checked 2026-06-09. Oracle AI Database 26ai (formerly 23ai) became GA on-premises for Linux x86-64 in Jan 2026; it continues the internal 23.x version line (e.g. 23.26.x quarterly release updates).

database-type.comment: Primary model relational; multi-model with document, graph, spatial and vector capabilities.

query-language.comment: SQL plus the proprietary PL/SQL procedural extension; SQL is the primary query interface.

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID transactions by default (multi-version read consistency, undo/redo durability).

schema-model.comment: Schema-enforced relational tables; flexible JSON columns are also supported but the default model is fixed-schema.

horizontal-scaling.comment: Native sharding (Oracle Sharding) and Real Application Clusters distribute data/load across nodes without third-party tooling.

replication.comment: Primary-replica via Data Guard/Active Data Guard; multi-primary via GoldenGate and the globally distributed (RAFT) database.

clustering.comment: Real Application Clusters (RAC) provides built-in shared-database clustering.

cloud-offering.comment: Official managed services: Oracle Autonomous Database and Oracle Base Database on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

full-text-search.comment: Built-in Oracle Text provides full-text indexing and search.

json-support.comment: Native JSON data type (since 12c, expanded in 21c/23ai) with full SQL/JSON querying and JSON Relational Duality.

geospatial.comment: Oracle Spatial provides native geospatial data types and queries.

time-series.comment: No dedicated time-series engine; time-based data is handled with standard tables/partitioning rather than a specialized time-series feature.

stored-procedures.comment: PL/SQL stored procedures (since Oracle7, 1992); also Java stored procedures.

official-gui.comment: Oracle SQL Developer and Enterprise Manager are official graphical tools.

cli-tool.comment: SQL*Plus and SQLcl are official command-line interfaces.

docker-image.comment: Oracle ships official container images via Oracle Container Registry (container-registry.oracle.com) and the oracle/docker-images repo, but these are not part of Docker Hub's curated Docker Official Images program. Strict reading of the attribute = false.

primary-language.comment: Implemented primarily in C and C++ with some assembly, per the Wikipedia infobox.

github-stars.comment: Closed-source proprietary product with no public source repository; per RESEARCH.md, GitHub stars are null for closed-source databases.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Stack Overflow and its API are not fetchable in this environment, so the [oracle]/[oracle-database] tag count could not be verified against a primary source; left null rather than guessed.

db-engines-rank.comment: Ranked #1 on DB-Engines as of the June 2026 ranking (checked 2026-06-09).

learning-curve.comment: Standard SQL lowers entry, but the vast feature set, complex administration (RAC, tablespaces, init parameters), and intricate licensing make it relatively steep to master; rated 2/5 per the Assessment Guidelines.
